item.title: [Advances in clinical management of acute severe ulcerative colitis].
item.description: Zhejiang da xue xue bao. Yi xue ban = Journal of Zhejiang University. Medical sciences - Acute severe ulcerative colitis (ASUC) is a critical emergency in inflam-matory bowel disease. Some patients do not respond to first-line intravenous corticosteroid therapy and require timely salva...

item.title: Identification of Symptom Clusters and Core Symptoms in Inflammatory Bowel Disease: A Network Analysis in Chinese Cohorts.
item.description: Gastroenterology research and practice - Research on symptom clustering in patients with inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) using network analysis is limited. Core symptom clusters in Chinese patients with IBD have not been clearly defined,...

item.title: Attomolar fecal cytokine profiling reveals gut immune dynamics and disease states.
item.description: bioRxiv : the preprint server for biology - The gut modulates systemic health, influencing immune, neurological, and cardiovascular processes. While fecal sequencing of microbial nucleic acids provides a non-invasive view of microbial compos...

item.title: Risk of avoidant/restrictive food intake disorders in patients with inflammatory bowel disease: a matched cross-sectional case-control study.
item.description: Journal of Crohn's & colitis - Individuals with inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) often modify their diet to manage symptoms; however, these behaviors may evolve into eating disorders, including avoidant/restrictive food intake d...

item.title: Beyond Nutrients: NOVA-Defined Dietary Patterns in Crohn's Disease and Healthy Adults.
item.description: Nutrients - RCT - Background: Diet quality and food processing patterns are increasingly recognized as important determinants of Crohn's disease (CD) risk and disease outcomes; however, direct comparisons with healt...
